caption: The video appears to be a presentation or lecture focusing on three key topics related to neuroscience and cognitive biases. The first topic, "Characterize (unconscious) bias," suggests an exploration of how unconscious biases can influence human behavior and decision-making. The second topic, "Neural Focus Groups," likely delves into the use of neuroscientific methods to understand group dynamics and collective responses, possibly through brain imaging techniques. The third topic, "Neuro-diagnostics," indicates a discussion on diagnostic tools and methods used in neuroscience to assess and analyze neural activity and brain function. The presenter seems to be guiding the audience through these concepts, possibly using slides or visual aids to enhance understanding. The consistent focus on these three points across the frames suggests a structured and detailed examination of each area within the broader context of neuroscience and cognitive science.
